intent: Open the reverse interview for a junior candidate with a focus on growth, learning, and onboarding — the questions a curious junior should ask.
primaryQuestion: Now it is your turn. What questions do you have for us about the role, the team, or how engineers grow here?
expectedSignals: Asks about onboarding structure: ramp-up period, first-90-days expectations, mentorship
Asks about learning culture: code review practices, pair programming, knowledge sharing
Asks about feedback cycles: how performance is assessed, how often, and by whom
Shows genuine curiosity about day-to-day work, not just salary or remote policy
redFlags: No questions at all — did not prepare anything to ask
Only asks about salary, benefits, or working hours
Questions are generic and could apply to any company without any research

scoringHints.description: Specific, thoughtful questions about growth and learning that show the candidate researched the role and company
Has questions but they are general — could apply to any company, no sign of research
No questions prepared, or only logistical questions about compensation and hours

followUps.question: Is there anything specific about how junior engineers develop here that you would like to understand?
followUps.purpose: Prompt a more focused growth-oriented question

followUps.question: What aspect of your career development would you want to make sure this role supports?
followUps.purpose: Invite the candidate to connect their questions to their own goals

en.title: Reverse interview — junior: growth and learning
en.displayQuestion: Now it is your turn. What questions do you have about the role, the team, or how engineers grow here?
en.displayIntent: Invite the candidate to ask questions; evaluate the curiosity and preparation behind their questions.
guidance: Ask about onboarding, mentorship, and learning culture.
Show you have researched the role, not just reciting generic questions.
Connect your questions to your own career goals.
commonMistakes: Asking only about salary and benefits.
Having no questions at all.
Asking generic questions that show no research.

vi.title: Phỏng vấn ngược — junior: phát triển và học hỏi
vi.displayQuestion: Bây giờ là lượt của bạn. Bạn có câu hỏi gì về vị trí, team, hoặc cách kỹ sư phát triển ở đây không?
vi.displayIntent: Mời ứng viên đặt câu hỏi; đánh giá sự tò mò và chuẩn bị đằng sau câu hỏi của họ.
guidance: Hỏi về onboarding, mentorship và văn hóa học hỏi.
Thể hiện bạn đã tìm hiểu về vị trí, không chỉ hỏi câu hỏi chung chung.
Kết nối câu hỏi với mục tiêu nghề nghiệp của bạn.
commonMistakes: Chỉ hỏi về lương và phúc lợi.
Không có câu hỏi nào.
Câu hỏi chung chung không cho thấy đã tìm hiểu.

ja.title: 逆面接 — ジュニア：成長と学習
ja.displayQuestion: では、あなたの番です。このポジション、チーム、またはエンジニアがここでどのように成長するかについて質問はありますか？
ja.displayIntent: 候補者に質問を促し、その質問の背後にある好奇心と準備を評価する。
guidance: オンボーディング、メンタリング、学習文化について質問する。
リサーチしたことを示す質問をする — 一般的な質問を繰り返すだけでない。
質問を自分のキャリア目標に結びつける。
commonMistakes: 給与と福利厚生だけを聞く。
全く質問がない。
リサーチを示さない一般的な質問をする。

intent: Open the reverse interview for a junior backend candidate — specifically invite questions about technical practices, the stack, and how they will learn the codebase.
primaryQuestion: Feel free to ask anything about our engineering practices, the tech stack you would be working with, or how new engineers get up to speed on the codebase.
expectedSignals: Asks about the current tech stack in production and the direction it is heading
Asks about code review culture: how thorough, how often, who reviews junior work
Asks how new engineers learn the codebase: documentation, pairing, shadowing
Shows awareness that learning the existing system is as important as writing new code
redFlags: No questions about the technical environment they will work in
Only asks about technology choices without connecting to how they will learn them
Asks to confirm the stack is the same as listed in the job description — no deeper curiosity

scoringHints.description: Specific questions about technical practices, learning path for the codebase, and engineering culture — shows genuine technical curiosity
Asks about the tech stack but stops there — no questions about practices or learning structure
No technical questions; only logistics or compensation questions

followUps.question: Is there a particular part of our stack or engineering practices you are curious to learn more about?
followUps.purpose: Encourage a more specific technical question

followUps.question: What would help you most in the first month to feel productive on this codebase?
followUps.purpose: Invite the candidate to express what onboarding support they need

en.title: Reverse interview — junior backend: tech stack and learning
en.displayQuestion: Feel free to ask about our engineering practices, the tech stack, or how new engineers get up to speed on the codebase.
en.displayIntent: Evaluate the candidate's technical curiosity and awareness of what they need to onboard successfully.
guidance: Ask about the tech stack and where it is heading.
Ask about code review and how junior work is supported.
Ask how new engineers learn the existing codebase.
commonMistakes: No technical questions at all.
Asking only to confirm what was already in the job description.
No curiosity about engineering practices or team support.

vi.title: Phỏng vấn ngược — junior backend: tech stack và học hỏi
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn có thể hỏi về quy trình kỹ thuật, tech stack, hoặc cách kỹ sư mới làm quen với codebase.
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá sự tò mò kỹ thuật và nhận thức của ứng viên về những gì họ cần để onboard thành công.
guidance: Hỏi về tech stack và hướng phát triển của nó.
Hỏi về code review và cách hỗ trợ công việc của junior.
Hỏi cách kỹ sư mới học codebase hiện có.
commonMistakes: Không có câu hỏi kỹ thuật nào.
Chỉ hỏi để xác nhận những gì đã có trong mô tả công việc.
Không tò mò về quy trình kỹ thuật hoặc hỗ trợ từ team.

ja.title: 逆面接 — ジュニアバックエンド：技術スタックと学習
ja.displayQuestion: エンジニアリング慣行、技術スタック、または新しいエンジニアがコードベースを習得する方法について自由に質問してください。
ja.displayIntent: 候補者の技術的な好奇心と、成功したオンボーディングに何が必要かの認識を評価する。
guidance: 技術スタックとその方向性について質問する。
コードレビューとジュニアの作業がどのようにサポートされるかを尋ねる。
新しいエンジニアが既存のコードベースをどのように学ぶかを尋ねる。
commonMistakes: 技術的な質問が全くない。
求人票に既に書いてあることを確認するだけ。
エンジニアリング慣行やチームのサポートへの関心がない。

intent: Open the reverse interview for a mid-level candidate — specifically invite questions about ownership, decision-making scope, and how impact is measured.
primaryQuestion: Now it is your turn. What would you like to understand about how engineers at this level own features, make decisions, and measure their impact here?
expectedSignals: Asks about the scope of technical ownership: how much autonomy an engineer has over design decisions
Asks how success is defined and measured for an engineer at this level
Asks about the product lifecycle: how ideas move from proposal to production
Shows interest in contributing beyond execution — wants to understand decision-making culture
redFlags: No questions about ownership or decision-making scope
Only asks about process tools (Jira, standups) without asking about influence and autonomy
Questions suggest they expect only to be assigned tasks with no independent ownership

scoringHints.description: Specific questions about ownership scope, how impact is measured, and how technical decisions are made — shows readiness for mid-level responsibility
Has questions but focused on process rather than ownership and impact
Questions focused only on logistics; no sign of interest in ownership or driving outcomes

followUps.question: What level of technical ownership are you looking for in your next role?
followUps.purpose: Invite the candidate to articulate what ownership means to them

followUps.question: Is there a specific type of project or problem scope you want to understand better about this team?
followUps.purpose: Redirect toward more substantive questions about role impact

en.title: Reverse interview — mid-level: ownership and impact
en.displayQuestion: What would you like to understand about how engineers at this level own features, make decisions, and measure their impact here?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate whether the candidate is thinking about ownership and impact, not just task execution.
guidance: Ask about your scope of technical ownership and autonomy.
Ask how success is defined and measured at this level.
Show interest in contributing to decisions, not just executing them.
commonMistakes: Asking only about process tools and daily routines.
No questions about ownership scope or decision-making culture.
Questions suggest an expectation of only being assigned tasks.

vi.title: Phỏng vấn ngược — mid-level: ownership và impact
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn muốn hiểu gì về cách kỹ sư ở level này sở hữu tính năng, đưa ra quyết định và đo lường tác động ở đây?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá xem ứng viên có đang suy nghĩ về ownership và impact, hay chỉ thực thi task.
guidance: Hỏi về phạm vi ownership kỹ thuật và quyền tự chủ.
Hỏi cách xác định và đo lường thành công ở level này.
Thể hiện sự quan tâm đến việc đóng góp vào quyết định, không chỉ thực thi.
commonMistakes: Chỉ hỏi về công cụ và quy trình hàng ngày.
Không có câu hỏi về phạm vi ownership hoặc văn hóa ra quyết định.
Câu hỏi cho thấy kỳ vọng chỉ được giao task.

ja.title: 逆面接 — ミドルレベル：オーナーシップとインパクト
ja.displayQuestion: このレベルのエンジニアがここでどのように機能を担当し、意思決定を行い、インパクトを測定するかについて知りたいことはありますか？
ja.displayIntent: 候補者がタスク実行だけでなく、オーナーシップとインパクトについて考えているかを評価する。
guidance: 技術的なオーナーシップの範囲と自律性について質問する。
このレベルでの成功の定義と測定方法を尋ねる。
決定を実行するだけでなく、貢献することへの関心を示す。
commonMistakes: プロセスツールと日常業務についてのみ質問する。
オーナーシップの範囲や意思決定文化について質問しない。
質問がタスクを割り当てられるだけを期待していることを示す。

intent: Open the reverse interview for a mid-level backend candidate — invite questions about technical architecture, engineering challenges, and how technical decisions are made.
primaryQuestion: What questions do you have about the technical architecture we work with, the engineering challenges the team is tackling, or how technical decisions get made here?
expectedSignals: Asks about a specific architectural challenge or trade-off the team is dealing with
Asks how technical decisions are made: who proposes, who reviews, how consensus is reached
Asks about the team's current pain points or technical debt
Shows understanding that backend systems have real constraints — does not assume everything is greenfield
redFlags: No questions about the actual technical environment
Questions are purely about technology choices without asking about engineering culture or constraints
Assumes the team works on greenfield with no legacy concerns

scoringHints.description: Specific, informed questions about architecture, technical challenges, or decision-making culture — shows system-level thinking
Asks about tech choices but not about the harder problems or decision processes
No technical questions or purely surface-level queries about the stack

followUps.question: What specific technical challenge or constraint would you want to understand better before joining?
followUps.purpose: Invite a more concrete technical question

followUps.question: Is there a particular architectural trade-off you are curious about in our system?

en.title: Reverse interview — mid backend: architecture and technical decisions
en.displayQuestion: What questions do you have about our technical architecture, engineering challenges, or how technical decisions get made?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate whether the candidate thinks beyond the tech stack and asks about real engineering constraints and decision culture.
guidance: Ask about specific architectural challenges the team faces.
Ask how technical decisions are proposed and approved.
Show awareness that systems have constraints, legacy, and trade-offs.
commonMistakes: Asking only what technologies are used without asking why.
No questions about engineering culture or decision-making.
Assuming a greenfield environment.

vi.title: Phỏng vấn ngược — mid backend: kiến trúc và quyết định kỹ thuật
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn có câu hỏi gì về kiến trúc kỹ thuật, thách thức kỹ thuật, hoặc cách quyết định kỹ thuật được đưa ra ở đây?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá xem ứng viên có suy nghĩ vượt ngoài tech stack và hỏi về các ràng buộc kỹ thuật thực tế và văn hóa ra quyết định.
guidance: Hỏi về các thách thức kiến trúc cụ thể mà team đang đối mặt.
Hỏi cách quyết định kỹ thuật được đề xuất và phê duyệt.
Thể hiện nhận thức rằng hệ thống có ràng buộc, legacy và trade-off.
commonMistakes: Chỉ hỏi sử dụng công nghệ gì mà không hỏi tại sao.
Không hỏi về văn hóa kỹ thuật hoặc ra quyết định.
Giả định môi trường greenfield.

ja.title: 逆面接 — ミドルバックエンド：アーキテクチャと技術的判断
ja.displayQuestion: 技術アーキテクチャ、エンジニアリングの課題、または技術的な意思決定の方法について質問はありますか？
ja.displayIntent: 候補者が技術スタックを超えて、実際のエンジニアリングの制約と意思決定文化について質問しているかを評価する。
guidance: チームが直面している特定のアーキテクチャ上の課題について質問する。
技術的な決定がどのように提案・承認されるかを尋ねる。
システムに制約、レガシー、トレードオフがあることを認識していることを示す。
commonMistakes: なぜそれを使うかを聞かずに、どの技術を使うかだけを聞く。
エンジニアリング文化や意思決定について質問しない。
グリーンフィールド環境を仮定する。

intent: Open the reverse interview for a senior candidate — invite questions about technical strategy, engineering autonomy, and team influence.
primaryQuestion: Now it is your turn. What would you like to understand about how senior engineers influence technical direction and contribute to the team here?
expectedSignals: Asks how senior engineers contribute to technical strategy and roadmap decisions
Asks about the culture of technical leadership: RFCs, ADRs, design reviews
Asks what the team's most important unsolved technical challenge is
Questions reflect a desire to drive outcomes, not just deliver tasks
redFlags: No questions about technical influence or leadership
Questions are at a junior level — about onboarding, tools, or daily routine
No curiosity about the team's strategic direction or hard problems

scoringHints.description: Strategic, informed questions about influence, technical leadership culture, and the team's hardest problems — shows senior-level thinking
Has good questions but they are more mid-level — about ownership but not about strategic influence
Questions are at the junior level: onboarding, tools, logistics, no strategic depth

followUps.question: What aspect of technical leadership or team influence is most important to you in your next role?
followUps.purpose: Invite a more substantive senior-level question

followUps.question: What is the hardest technical problem you hope to work on here?
followUps.purpose: Encourage the candidate to show they have thought about the team's challenges

en.title: Reverse interview — senior: technical strategy and influence
en.displayQuestion: What would you like to understand about how senior engineers influence technical direction and contribute to the team here?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate whether the candidate thinks about technical leadership, strategy, and influence — not just execution.
guidance: Ask how senior engineers contribute to technical strategy.
Ask about the team's hardest unsolved technical problem.
Show you are thinking about driving outcomes, not just delivering tasks.
commonMistakes: Asking junior-level questions about tools and onboarding.
No questions about technical influence or strategic direction.
No curiosity about the team's most important challenges.

vi.title: Phỏng vấn ngược — senior: chiến lược kỹ thuật và ảnh hưởng
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn muốn hiểu gì về cách kỹ sư senior ảnh hưởng đến định hướng kỹ thuật và đóng góp cho team ở đây?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá xem ứng viên có suy nghĩ về kỹ năng lãnh đạo kỹ thuật, chiến lược và ảnh hưởng — không chỉ thực thi.
guidance: Hỏi cách kỹ sư senior đóng góp vào chiến lược kỹ thuật.
Hỏi về vấn đề kỹ thuật khó nhất chưa được giải quyết của team.
Thể hiện bạn đang suy nghĩ về việc tạo ra kết quả, không chỉ hoàn thành task.
commonMistakes: Hỏi câu hỏi cấp junior về công cụ và onboarding.
Không hỏi về ảnh hưởng kỹ thuật hoặc định hướng chiến lược.
Không tò mò về thách thức quan trọng nhất của team.

ja.title: 逆面接 — シニア：技術戦略と影響力
ja.displayQuestion: シニアエンジニアがここでどのように技術的な方向性に影響を与え、チームに貢献するかについて知りたいことはありますか？
ja.displayIntent: 候補者が単なる実行だけでなく、技術的リーダーシップ、戦略、影響力について考えているかを評価する。
guidance: シニアエンジニアが技術戦略にどのように貢献するかを質問する。
チームの最も困難な未解決の技術的問題について尋ねる。
タスクをこなすだけでなく、成果を推進することを考えていることを示す。
commonMistakes: ツールやオンボーディングについてジュニアレベルの質問をする。
技術的な影響力や戦略的方向性について質問しない。
チームの最重要課題への関心がない。

intent: Open the reverse interview for a senior backend candidate — invite questions about system architecture, engineering evolution, and where senior engineers can make the biggest impact.
primaryQuestion: What questions do you have about our system architecture, the biggest technical bets we are making, or where a senior backend engineer can have the most impact here?
expectedSignals: Asks about architectural decisions the team is currently debating or recently made
Asks about the system's biggest pain points and what is being done about them
Asks where the team needs the most senior engineering leverage: design, mentorship, reliability
Shows depth — not just asking about the stack but about the hard problems and trade-offs
redFlags: Questions are limited to confirming the tech stack or tooling
No questions about the team's hard engineering problems
No curiosity about where their skills would be most valuable or how they could make the biggest contribution

scoringHints.description: Incisive questions about architecture, real trade-offs, and where senior impact matters most — demonstrates system-level thinking
Good questions about the tech environment but missing the strategic depth of a senior candidate
Questions match a junior or mid profile — stack confirmation, onboarding, tools

followUps.question: What area of the system would you most want to understand before deciding to join?
Is there a specific architectural decision or trade-off you are curious about in our system?

en.title: Reverse interview — senior backend: architecture and impact
en.displayQuestion: What questions do you have about our system architecture, the biggest technical bets we are making, or where a senior backend engineer can have the most impact?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate whether the senior candidate asks about real architectural challenges and where they can make the biggest contribution.
guidance: Ask about current architectural debates or decisions.
Ask about the system's most critical pain points.
Show you are thinking about where your skills add the most leverage.
commonMistakes: Only confirming the tech stack without asking about challenges.
No questions about hard engineering problems or trade-offs.
No curiosity about where senior impact would be most valuable.

vi.title: Phỏng vấn ngược — senior backend: kiến trúc và impact
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn có câu hỏi gì về kiến trúc hệ thống, các quyết định kỹ thuật lớn đang thực hiện, hoặc nơi kỹ sư backend senior có thể tạo ra tác động lớn nhất ở đây?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá xem ứng viên senior có hỏi về các thách thức kiến trúc thực sự và nơi họ có thể đóng góp nhiều nhất.
guidance: Hỏi về các tranh luận hoặc quyết định kiến trúc hiện tại.
Hỏi về điểm đau quan trọng nhất của hệ thống.
Thể hiện bạn đang suy nghĩ về nơi kỹ năng của bạn tạo ra đòn bẩy lớn nhất.
commonMistakes: Chỉ xác nhận tech stack mà không hỏi về thách thức.
Không hỏi về các vấn đề kỹ thuật khó hoặc trade-off.
Không tò mò về nơi senior impact quan trọng nhất.

ja.title: 逆面接 — シニアバックエンド：アーキテクチャとインパクト
ja.displayQuestion: システムアーキテクチャ、最大の技術的な賭け、またはシニアバックエンドエンジニアが最も大きなインパクトを与えられる場所について質問はありますか？
ja.displayIntent: シニア候補者が実際のアーキテクチャ上の課題と最大の貢献ができる場所について質問しているかを評価する。
guidance: 現在のアーキテクチャ上の議論や決定について質問する。
システムの最も重要なペインポイントを尋ねる。
自分のスキルが最も活かせる場所を考えていることを示す。
commonMistakes: 課題を尋ねずに技術スタックを確認するだけ。
困難なエンジニアリングの問題やトレードオフについて質問しない。
シニアとしての貢献が最も価値を持つ場所への関心がない。

intent: Generic reverse interview opener for any level — invite questions about engineering process, delivery practices, and how the team operates day to day.
primaryQuestion: Please go ahead and ask any questions you have — about our engineering process, how the team works, or anything else you would want to know before making a decision.
expectedSignals: Has at least one substantive question that goes beyond logistics
Questions show the candidate has thought about what matters to them in a team environment
At least one question demonstrates research into the company, role, or team
The candidate is engaged and curious, not passive
redFlags: No questions at all — or only 'when will I hear back?'
All questions are about salary, benefits, and work hours
Generic questions with no sign of research or genuine curiosity

scoringHints.description: Thoughtful, specific questions that show research and genuine interest in the role and team
Has questions but they are generic and could apply to any company
No real questions, or only logistical questions with no substance

followUps.question: Is there anything specific about how we work that would help you decide if this is the right fit for you?

en.title: Reverse interview — general: engineering process and team
en.displayQuestion: Please go ahead and ask any questions you have about our engineering process, how the team works, or anything else before you decide.
en.displayIntent: Evaluate the candidate's curiosity, preparation, and genuine interest in the role.
guidance: Ask at least one question that shows you researched the company or role.
Focus on what matters most to you in a team environment.
Go beyond logistics — show genuine engagement.
commonMistakes: Having no questions.
Asking only about salary and working hours.
Generic questions that apply to any company.

vi.title: Phỏng vấn ngược — chung: quy trình kỹ thuật và team
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn có thể đặt câu hỏi về quy trình kỹ thuật, cách team làm việc, hoặc bất cứ điều gì trước khi đưa ra quyết định.
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá sự tò mò, chuẩn bị và sự quan tâm thực sự của ứng viên đối với vị trí.
guidance: Đặt ít nhất một câu hỏi cho thấy bạn đã tìm hiểu về công ty hoặc vị trí.
Tập trung vào điều quan trọng nhất với bạn trong môi trường team.
Vượt ra ngoài logistics — thể hiện sự tham gia thực sự.
commonMistakes: Không có câu hỏi.
Chỉ hỏi về lương và giờ làm việc.
Câu hỏi chung chung áp dụng cho bất kỳ công ty nào.

ja.title: 逆面接 — 一般：エンジニアリングプロセスとチーム
ja.displayQuestion: エンジニアリングプロセス、チームの働き方、または決定する前に知りたいことについて、自由に質問してください。
ja.displayIntent: 候補者の好奇心、準備、そしてロールへの真摯な関心を評価する。
guidance: 会社やロールを調査したことを示す質問を少なくとも一つする。
チーム環境で最も重要なことに焦点を当てる。
ロジスティクスを超えて — 真の関与を示す。
commonMistakes: 質問がない。
給与と勤務時間だけを聞く。
どの会社にも当てはまる一般的な質問をする。

intent: Generic reverse interview opener focused on team dynamics — invite questions about collaboration, conflict resolution, and how the team handles difficult situations.
primaryQuestion: What would you like to know about the team you would be joining — how we collaborate, how we handle disagreements, or what makes this team work well?
expectedSignals: Asks about team collaboration style: how work is planned, who is involved in decisions
Asks how the team handles disagreements or technical conflicts
Asks about what makes the team effective or what the team is actively trying to improve
Shows the candidate values team culture, not just individual output
redFlags: No questions about team dynamics or collaboration
Questions imply the candidate expects to work independently with no team interaction
No curiosity about how the team handles hard situations like disagreements or failure

scoringHints.description: Thoughtful questions about team culture, collaboration, and how hard situations are handled — shows collaborative mindset
Has team-related questions but they are surface level
No team-focused questions; candidate appears indifferent to team dynamics

followUps.question: What does a healthy team dynamic look like to you, and what would you want to understand about ours?
followUps.purpose: Encourage a more specific team-culture question

en.title: Reverse interview — general: team dynamics and collaboration
en.displayQuestion: What would you like to know about the team you would be joining — how we collaborate, handle disagreements, or what makes the team work well?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate whether the candidate is curious about team dynamics and collaboration culture.
guidance: Ask how the team plans work and makes decisions together.
Ask how disagreements or technical conflicts are resolved.
Show you value team culture, not just individual output.
commonMistakes: No questions about team dynamics.
Implying you prefer to work entirely independently.
No curiosity about how the team handles hard situations.

vi.title: Phỏng vấn ngược — chung: team dynamics và collaboration
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn muốn biết gì về team bạn sẽ gia nhập — cách chúng tôi collaborate, xử lý bất đồng, hoặc điều gì khiến team hoạt động tốt?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá xem ứng viên có tò mò về team dynamics và văn hóa collaboration không.
guidance: Hỏi cách team lên kế hoạch công việc và đưa ra quyết định cùng nhau.
Hỏi cách giải quyết bất đồng hoặc xung đột kỹ thuật.
Thể hiện bạn coi trọng văn hóa team, không chỉ output cá nhân.
commonMistakes: Không có câu hỏi về team dynamics.
Ngụ ý thích làm việc hoàn toàn độc lập.
Không tò mò về cách team xử lý tình huống khó.

ja.title: 逆面接 — 一般：チームダイナミクスとコラボレーション
ja.displayQuestion: 参加するチームについて知りたいことはありますか — どのようにコラボレーションし、意見の相違をどう処理し、チームがうまく機能する理由は何でしょうか？
ja.displayIntent: 候補者がチームダイナミクスとコラボレーション文化に関心があるかを評価する。
guidance: チームがどのように一緒に仕事を計画し、意思決定するかを尋ねる。
意見の相違や技術的な衝突がどのように解決されるかを尋ねる。
個人のアウトプットだけでなく、チーム文化を大切にしていることを示す。
commonMistakes: チームダイナミクスについての質問がない。
完全に独立して働くことを好むことを暗示する。
チームが困難な状況にどう対処するかへの関心がない。

intent: Invite mid and senior candidates to ask about the product roadmap, business context, and how engineering connects to product outcomes.
primaryQuestion: What questions do you have about the product we are building, the business problems we are solving, or how engineering and product work together here?
expectedSignals: Asks about the product's current biggest challenge or opportunity
Asks how engineering roadmap is prioritized alongside product roadmap
Shows interest in understanding the business context, not just the technical scope
Asks about user feedback loops: how does engineering learn what users actually need
redFlags: No questions about the product or business context
Questions show the candidate sees engineering as entirely separate from business outcomes
No curiosity about how technical decisions connect to user or business impact

scoringHints.description: Specific questions about product-engineering collaboration, business priorities, and user feedback — shows product-aware engineering mindset
Some product interest but questions are surface level
Engineering-only mindset — no questions about product, users, or business impact

followUps.question: What would you want to understand about the product or business problem before deciding to join?
followUps.purpose: Prompt the candidate to connect their work to business outcomes

en.title: Reverse interview — mid/senior: product and business context
en.displayQuestion: What questions do you have about the product we are building, the business problems we are solving, or how engineering and product work together?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate whether the candidate thinks about engineering in the context of business and product outcomes.
guidance: Ask about the product's biggest challenge or opportunity right now.
Ask how engineering and product roadmaps are aligned.
Show interest in understanding the user and business context.
commonMistakes: No questions about product or business impact.
Treating engineering as completely separate from business.
No curiosity about user feedback or product direction.

vi.title: Phỏng vấn ngược — mid/senior: bối cảnh sản phẩm và kinh doanh
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn có câu hỏi gì về sản phẩm chúng tôi đang xây dựng, vấn đề kinh doanh đang giải quyết, hoặc cách kỹ thuật và sản phẩm phối hợp ở đây?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá xem ứng viên có suy nghĩ về kỹ thuật trong bối cảnh kết quả kinh doanh và sản phẩm.
guidance: Hỏi về thách thức hoặc cơ hội lớn nhất của sản phẩm hiện tại.
Hỏi cách roadmap kỹ thuật và sản phẩm được đồng bộ.
Thể hiện sự quan tâm đến việc hiểu bối cảnh người dùng và kinh doanh.
commonMistakes: Không có câu hỏi về sản phẩm hoặc tác động kinh doanh.
Xem kỹ thuật hoàn toàn tách biệt với kinh doanh.
Không tò mò về phản hồi người dùng hoặc định hướng sản phẩm.

ja.title: 逆面接 — ミドル/シニア：プロダクトとビジネスコンテキスト
ja.displayQuestion: 構築しているプロダクト、解決しているビジネス課題、またはエンジニアリングとプロダクトの連携方法について質問はありますか？
ja.displayIntent: 候補者がビジネスとプロダクトの成果のコンテキストでエンジニアリングを考えているかを評価する。
guidance: 現在のプロダクトの最大の課題や機会について質問する。
エンジニアリングとプロダクトのロードマップがどのように整合されているかを尋ねる。
ユーザーとビジネスのコンテキストを理解することへの関心を示す。
commonMistakes: プロダクトやビジネスインパクトについての質問がない。
エンジニアリングをビジネスから完全に切り離して考える。
ユーザーフィードバックやプロダクト方向性への関心がない。

intent: Invite mid and senior backend/devops candidates to ask about deployment practices, on-call culture, and production reliability.
primaryQuestion: What questions do you have about how we deploy, how we handle production incidents, or what our on-call and reliability culture looks like?
expectedSignals: Asks about deployment frequency and pipeline: CI/CD, feature flags, canary releases
Asks about on-call structure: who is on-call, how incidents are managed, blameless postmortems
Asks about the team's approach to reliability: SLOs, error budgets, observability tooling
Questions suggest the candidate takes production responsibility seriously
redFlags: No questions about production operations or deployment
Questions imply the candidate expects someone else to handle production concerns
No curiosity about on-call, incidents, or reliability practices

scoringHints.description: Specific questions about deployment, on-call structure, and reliability culture — shows operational maturity
Some deployment or on-call questions but not operationally deep
No production operations questions; candidate seems unaware of or uninterested in operational responsibility

followUps.question: What would you want to know about our incident process or on-call culture before deciding to join?

en.title: Reverse interview — mid/senior backend: deployment and reliability
en.displayQuestion: What questions do you have about how we deploy, handle production incidents, or what our on-call and reliability culture looks like?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate operational maturity and awareness of production responsibility.
guidance: Ask about deployment frequency and your CI/CD pipeline.
Ask how incidents are handled and whether you do blameless postmortems.
Show you take production responsibility seriously.
commonMistakes: No questions about deployment or production operations.
Implying someone else handles production concerns.
No curiosity about on-call or reliability.

vi.title: Phỏng vấn ngược — mid/senior backend: deployment và reliability
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn có câu hỏi gì về cách chúng tôi deploy, xử lý production incident, hoặc văn hóa on-call và reliability như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá sự trưởng thành về mặt vận hành và nhận thức về trách nhiệm production.
guidance: Hỏi về tần suất deploy và CI/CD pipeline.
Hỏi cách xử lý incident và có blameless postmortem không.
Thể hiện bạn coi trọng trách nhiệm production.
commonMistakes: Không có câu hỏi về deployment hoặc vận hành production.
Ngụ ý người khác xử lý các vấn đề production.
Không tò mò về on-call hoặc reliability.

ja.title: 逆面接 — ミドル/シニアバックエンド：デプロイと信頼性
ja.displayQuestion: デプロイ方法、本番インシデントの処理、またはオンコールと信頼性の文化について質問はありますか？
ja.displayIntent: 運用成熟度と本番責任への認識を評価する。
guidance: デプロイ頻度とCI/CDパイプラインについて質問する。
インシデントの処理方法とブレームレスポストモーテムについて尋ねる。
本番責任を真剣に考えていることを示す。
commonMistakes: デプロイや本番運用についての質問がない。
本番に関する懸念を他の誰かが処理することを暗示する。
オンコールや信頼性への関心がない。

intent: Invite mid and senior frontend candidates to ask about frontend architecture, design system, performance culture, and cross-team collaboration.
primaryQuestion: What questions do you have about our frontend architecture, the design system we work with, how we approach performance, or how frontend and backend collaborate here?
expectedSignals: Asks about state management strategy and how complex UI state is handled
Asks about the design system: component library ownership, designer-developer handoff
Asks about frontend performance culture: bundle size budgets, Core Web Vitals, monitoring
Asks how frontend and backend API contracts are designed and evolved together
redFlags: No questions about frontend architecture or cross-team collaboration
Questions only confirm the framework or library being used
No curiosity about design systems, performance, or frontend engineering culture

scoringHints.description: Specific questions about architecture, performance culture, design-dev workflow, and API collaboration — shows frontend engineering depth
Has frontend questions but limited to framework or library choice
No meaningful frontend architecture questions; only framework confirmation

followUps.question: What would you want to understand about how we handle frontend performance or our design system before joining?

en.title: Reverse interview — mid/senior frontend: architecture and collaboration
en.displayQuestion: What questions do you have about our frontend architecture, design system, performance culture, or how frontend and backend collaborate?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate frontend engineering depth and interest in cross-functional collaboration.
guidance: Ask about state management approach and how complex UI state is handled.
Ask about design system ownership and the designer-developer workflow.
Show interest in frontend performance culture and API collaboration.
commonMistakes: Only confirming what framework is used.
No questions about cross-team collaboration.
No curiosity about performance culture or design systems.

vi.title: Phỏng vấn ngược — mid/senior frontend: kiến trúc và collaboration
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn có câu hỏi gì về kiến trúc frontend, design system, văn hóa performance, hoặc cách frontend và backend phối hợp ở đây?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá độ sâu kỹ thuật frontend và sự quan tâm đến collaboration liên chức năng.
guidance: Hỏi về cách quản lý state và xử lý UI state phức tạp.
Hỏi về ownership của design system và quy trình làm việc giữa designer và developer.
Thể hiện sự quan tâm đến văn hóa performance frontend và collaboration API.
commonMistakes: Chỉ xác nhận framework đang dùng.
Không có câu hỏi về collaboration liên team.
Không tò mò về văn hóa performance hoặc design system.

ja.title: 逆面接 — ミドル/シニアフロントエンド：アーキテクチャとコラボレーション
ja.displayQuestion: フロントエンドアーキテクチャ、デザインシステム、パフォーマンス文化、またはフロントエンドとバックエンドの連携方法について質問はありますか？
ja.displayIntent: フロントエンドエンジニアリングの深さとクロスファンクショナルなコラボレーションへの関心を評価する。
guidance: 状態管理のアプローチと複雑なUI状態の処理方法について質問する。
デザインシステムのオーナーシップとデザイナー・デベロッパーのワークフローを尋ねる。
フロントエンドパフォーマンス文化とAPIコラボレーションへの関心を示す。
commonMistakes: 使用しているフレームワークを確認するだけ。
チーム間のコラボレーションについて質問しない。
パフォーマンス文化やデザインシステムへの関心がない。

intent: Generic reverse interview probe about company culture, values, and how decisions are made — applicable to any role and level.
primaryQuestion: What would you like to know about our company culture, how we make decisions, or what it is actually like to work here day to day?
expectedSignals: Asks about specific cultural values the company lives by — not just what is written on the website
Asks how decisions are made: top-down, consensus, data-driven
Asks what the company does when things go wrong: how failures are handled
Shows the candidate has thought about culture fit as a two-way evaluation
redFlags: No questions about culture or values
Questions show no awareness that culture fit goes both ways
Only logistical questions about remote work policy or office hours

scoringHints.description: Thoughtful questions about values, decision culture, and how the company handles failure — shows the candidate is evaluating fit, not just accepting the offer
Has culture questions but they are generic
No culture questions; or only remote/in-office logistics

followUps.question: Is there a specific aspect of company culture that is particularly important to you in choosing your next role?
followUps.purpose: Invite a more personal and specific cultural question

en.title: Reverse interview — general: company culture and values
en.displayQuestion: What would you like to know about our company culture, how we make decisions, or what it is actually like to work here?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate whether the candidate treats the interview as a two-way evaluation of cultural fit.
guidance: Ask about the values the company actually lives by, not just the ones on the website.
Ask how decisions are made and what happens when things go wrong.
Treat culture fit as a two-way evaluation.
commonMistakes: No culture-related questions at all.
Only asking about remote work policy or office hours.
Showing no awareness that culture fit is a two-way assessment.

vi.title: Phỏng vấn ngược — chung: văn hóa và giá trị công ty
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn muốn biết gì về văn hóa công ty, cách đưa ra quyết định, hoặc thực tế làm việc ở đây như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá xem ứng viên có coi cuộc phỏng vấn là đánh giá hai chiều về cultural fit.
guidance: Hỏi về những giá trị công ty thực sự sống theo, không chỉ những gì viết trên website.
Hỏi cách đưa ra quyết định và điều gì xảy ra khi mọi thứ không ổn.
Coi cultural fit là đánh giá hai chiều.
commonMistakes: Không có câu hỏi liên quan đến văn hóa.
Chỉ hỏi về chính sách remote work hoặc giờ làm việc.
Không nhận thức rằng cultural fit là đánh giá hai chiều.

ja.title: 逆面接 — 一般：企業文化と価値観
ja.displayQuestion: 企業文化、意思決定の方法、または実際にここで働くことがどのようなものかについて知りたいことはありますか？
ja.displayIntent: 候補者が面接を文化的適合性の双方向評価として扱っているかを評価する。
guidance: ウェブサイトに書いてあることだけでなく、会社が実際に体現している価値観について質問する。
意思決定の方法と物事がうまくいかないときに何が起こるかを尋ねる。
文化的適合性を双方向の評価として扱う。
commonMistakes: 文化に関する質問が全くない。
リモートワークポリシーや勤務時間だけを聞く。
文化的適合性が双方向の評価であることを認識しない。
